name: Mary-Claire King
bio: Mary-Claire King proved that breast cancer can be inherited through a single gene — BRCA1 — revolutionizing cancer research and prevention. Her discovery, made in 1990 after 17 years of research, enabled genetic testing that has saved countless lives through early detection. She also proved that humans and chimpanzees are 99% genetically identical and used DNA analysis to identify victims of Argentina's 'Dirty War,' reuniting stolen children with their biological families.
bioCn: 玛丽-克莱尔·金证明了乳腺癌可以通过单一基因——BRCA1——遗传，革新了癌症研究和预防。她的发现是在17年研究之后于1990年做出的，使基因检测成为可能，通过早期检测挽救了无数生命。她还证明人类和黑猩猩在基因上有99%相同，并利用DNA分析鉴定阿根廷'肮脏战争'的受害者，帮助被盗儿童与亲生家庭团聚。
achievements: Discovered BRCA1 breast cancer gene
Proved 99% genetic similarity between humans and chimps
Used DNA to identify victims of Argentina's Dirty War
achievementsCn: 发现BRCA1乳腺癌基因
证明人类和黑猩猩99%基因相似
利用DNA鉴定阿根廷肮脏战争受害者
references: King, M.C. et al. 'Breast and Ovarian Cancer Risks Due to Inherited Mutations in BRCA1 and BRCA2,' Science, 2003.

name: Katalin Karikó
bio: Katalin Karikó spent decades researching mRNA technology despite repeated rejections, demotions, and funding denials. Her persistence paid off spectacularly when her foundational work on modified mRNA enabled the rapid development of the Pfizer-BioNTech and Moderna COVID-19 vaccines, which have been administered billions of times worldwide. She received the 2023 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine, vindicating decades of underfunded, underappreciated research.
bioCn: 卡塔林·卡里科尽管一再遭到拒绝、降职和资金否决，仍花了数十年研究mRNA技术。她的坚持获得了惊人的回报——她在修饰mRNA方面的基础工作使辉瑞-BioNTech和Moderna新冠疫苗得以快速开发，这些疫苗已在全球接种数十亿次。她获得了2023年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖，证明了数十年来资金不足、不受重视的研究的价值。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2023)
Foundational mRNA research enabled COVID-19 vaccines
Vaccines administered billions of times worldwide
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（2023年）
基础mRNA研究使新冠疫苗成为可能
疫苗在全球接种数十亿次
quote: I thought of leaving science many times, but I always went back to the bench.
quoteCn: 我多次想过离开科学，但总是回到了实验台前。

name: Flossie Wong-Staal
tagline: First to Clone and Map HIV Genome
taglineCn: 首次克隆和绘制HIV基因组图谱
bio: Flossie Wong-Staal was the first scientist to clone HIV and determine the function of its genes, work that was essential for proving HIV causes AIDS and for developing the first HIV blood test. Born in Guangzhou, China, she moved to the United States and became one of the most cited scientists in the world. She later developed a novel approach to gene therapy and co-founded a biotech company focused on treating hepatitis C.
bioCn: 黄以静是第一位克隆HIV并确定其基因功能的科学家，这一工作对证明HIV导致AIDS以及开发第一个HIV血液检测至关重要。她出生于中国广州，移居美国后成为世界上被引用最多的科学家之一。她后来开发了一种新的基因治疗方法，并共同创办了一家专注于治疗丙型肝炎的生物技术公司。
achievements: First to clone HIV and map its genome
Work essential for first HIV blood test
Among most cited scientists in the world
achievementsCn: 首次克隆HIV并绘制其基因组图谱
工作对第一个HIV血液检测至关重要
世界上被引用最多的科学家之一

name: Patricia Bath
taglineCn: 发明了激光白内障手术
bio: Patricia Bath was an ophthalmologist and inventor who created the Laserphaco Probe, a device that uses laser technology to remove cataracts and restore sight. She was the first African-American woman to complete a residency in ophthalmology, the first to receive a medical patent, and the first African-American woman doctor to receive a patent for a medical purpose. Her invention has restored sight to people who had been blind for over 30 years.
bioCn: 帕特里夏·巴斯是一位眼科医生和发明家，她创造了Laserphaco探针——一种使用激光技术去除白内障并恢复视力的设备。她是第一位完成眼科住院医师培训的非裔美国女性，第一位获得医学专利的非裔美国女性，也是第一位因医疗目的获得专利的非裔美国女医生。她的发明已为失明超过30年的人恢复了视力。
achievements: Invented Laserphaco Probe for cataract surgery
First African-American woman with a medical patent
Restored sight to people blind for 30+ years
achievementsCn: 发明了用于白内障手术的Laserphaco探针
第一位获得医学专利的非裔美国女性
为失明超过30年的人恢复了视力
references: Bath, P. US Patent 4,744,360: Apparatus for ablating and removing cataract lenses, 1988.

name: Esther Lederberg
bio: Esther Lederberg made foundational discoveries in bacterial genetics, including the discovery of lambda phage (a virus that infects bacteria) and the invention of replica plating, a technique still used in microbiology labs worldwide. Despite these breakthrough contributions, the 1958 Nobel Prize for work on bacterial genetics was awarded only to her husband Joshua Lederberg. Her techniques remain essential tools in genetics and molecular biology research.
bioCn: 埃丝特·莱德伯格在细菌遗传学方面做出了奠基性发现，包括发现λ噬菌体（一种感染细菌的病毒）和发明复制平板法——一种至今仍在全世界微生物学实验室使用的技术。尽管她做出了突破性贡献，1958年细菌遗传学方面的诺贝尔奖仅授予了她的丈夫约书亚·莱德伯格。她的技术至今仍是遗传学和分子生物学研究的基本工具。
achievements: Discovered lambda phage
Invented replica plating technique
Foundational contributions to bacterial genetics
achievementsCn: 发现λ噬菌体
发明复制平板法
对细菌遗传学做出奠基性贡献

name: Elizabeth Blackburn
taglineCn: 发现端粒酶的诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Elizabeth Blackburn is a molecular biologist who discovered the molecular nature of telomeres — the protective caps at the ends of chromosomes — and co-discovered telomerase, the enzyme that replenishes them. This work revealed fundamental mechanisms of aging and cancer. She shared the 2009 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine with Carol Greider and Jack Szostak. Her research has opened vast new fields in aging biology, cancer research, and stress physiology. She served as President of the Salk Institute for Biological Studies.
bioCn: 伊丽莎白·布莱克本是一位分子生物学家，她发现了端粒——染色体末端保护帽——的分子本质，并共同发现了补充端粒的端粒酶。这项工作揭示了衰老和癌症的基本机制。她与卡罗尔·格雷德和杰克·绍斯塔克共同获得了2009年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖。她的研究开辟了衰老生物学、癌症研究和应激生理学的广阔新领域。她曾担任索尔克生物研究所所长。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2009)
Discovered the molecular nature of telomeres
Co-discovered telomerase enzyme
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（2009年）
发现端粒的分子本质
共同发现端粒酶
references: Blackburn, E. & Epel, E. The Telomere Effect. Grand Central Publishing, 2017.

name: Nancy Wexler
tagline: Led Discovery of the Huntington's Disease Gene
taglineCn: 领导发现亨廷顿病基因
bio: Nancy Wexler is a neuropsychologist who led the international effort to locate the gene responsible for Huntington's disease. Her groundbreaking research with a large Venezuelan kindred near Lake Maracaibo was crucial to mapping the gene to chromosome 4 in 1983 and ultimately identifying it in 1993. Her quest was deeply personal — her own mother died of Huntington's disease. As president of the Hereditary Disease Foundation, she has dedicated her career to understanding and combating hereditary diseases.
bioCn: 南希·韦克斯勒是一位神经心理学家，领导了定位亨廷顿病致病基因的国际研究。她对委内瑞拉马拉开波湖附近一个大家族的开创性研究，对1983年将该基因定位到第4号染色体以及1993年最终鉴定该基因起到了关键作用。她的探索有着深刻的个人原因——她的母亲死于亨廷顿病。作为遗传性疾病基金会主席，她毕生致力于理解和对抗遗传性疾病。
achievements: Led effort to locate the Huntington's disease gene
Research crucial to mapping gene to chromosome 4 (1983)
President of the Hereditary Disease Foundation
achievementsCn: 领导定位亨廷顿病基因的研究
研究对将基因定位到第4号染色体起关键作用（1983年）
遗传性疾病基金会主席
references: Wexler, A. Mapping Fate: A Memoir of Family, Risk, and Genetic Research. University of California Press, 1996.

name: Janet Rowley
taglineCn: 证明染色体易位可导致癌症
bio: Janet Rowley was a geneticist who discovered in 1972 that chromosomal translocations — where pieces of chromosomes swap places — can cause cancer, fundamentally changing our understanding of cancer genetics. She identified the specific translocation in the Philadelphia chromosome responsible for chronic myelogenous leukemia. Her work led directly to the development of imatinib (Gleevec), one of the first targeted cancer therapies. She received the Presidential Medal of Freedom in 2009 for her transformative contributions to medicine.
bioCn: 珍妮特·罗利是一位遗传学家，1972年发现染色体易位——即染色体片段互换位置——可以导致癌症，从根本上改变了人们对癌症遗传学的认识。她鉴定了慢性粒细胞白血病中费城染色体的特定易位。她的工作直接促成了伊马替尼（格列卫）的开发，这是最早的靶向癌症疗法之一。2009年，她因对医学的变革性贡献获得了总统自由勋章。
achievements: Discovered chromosomal translocations cause cancer (1972)
Identified Philadelphia chromosome translocation in leukemia
Presidential Medal of Freedom (2009)
achievementsCn: 发现染色体易位可导致癌症（1972年）
鉴定白血病中费城染色体的易位
总统自由勋章（2009年）
references: National Medal of Science and Presidential Medal of Freedom citations.

name: Carol Greider
taglineCn: 因发现端粒酶获诺贝尔奖
bio: Carol Greider is a molecular biologist who co-discovered telomerase, the enzyme that replenishes telomeres at chromosome ends, while still a graduate student in Elizabeth Blackburn's laboratory in 1984. This groundbreaking discovery revealed a fundamental mechanism of cellular aging and cancer biology. She shared the 2009 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine with Blackburn and Jack Szostak. Her continued research on telomere biology has profound implications for understanding aging, cancer, and age-related diseases.
bioCn: 卡罗尔·格雷德是一位分子生物学家，1984年还在伊丽莎白·布莱克本实验室攻读研究生时，就共同发现了端粒酶——一种补充染色体末端端粒的酶。这一突破性发现揭示了细胞衰老和癌症生物学的基本机制。她与布莱克本和杰克·绍斯塔克共同获得2009年诺贝尔生理学或医学奖。她在端粒生物学方面的持续研究对理解衰老、癌症和衰老相关疾病具有深远意义。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (2009)
Co-discovered telomerase as a graduate student (1984)
Revealed fundamental mechanisms of cellular aging
achievementsCn: 诺贝尔生理学或医学奖（2009年）
研究生期间共同发现端粒酶（1984年）
揭示细胞衰老的基本机制

name: Lydia Villa-Komaroff
nameCn: 莉迪亚·比利亚-科马罗夫
tagline: Pioneer in Bacterial Insulin Gene Cloning
taglineCn: 细菌胰岛素基因克隆先驱
bio: Lydia Villa-Komaroff is a Mexican-American molecular biologist who was part of the team that first demonstrated bacteria could be engineered to produce insulin in 1978, paving the way for mass production of synthetic insulin for diabetes treatment. She was one of the first Mexican-American women to earn a PhD in science. As a co-founder of the Society for the Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science (SACNAS), she has been a tireless advocate for diversity in STEM fields.
bioCn: 莉迪亚·比利亚-科马罗夫是一位墨西哥裔美国分子生物学家，1978年她所在的团队首次证明细菌可以被改造以生产胰岛素，为大规模生产合成胰岛素治疗糖尿病铺平了道路。她是最早获得科学博士学位的墨西哥裔美国女性之一。作为推动奇卡诺/拉丁裔和美洲原住民科学进步协会（SACNAS）的联合创始人，她一直是STEM领域多元化的不懈倡导者。
achievements: Part of team that first cloned insulin gene in bacteria (1978)
One of the first Mexican-American women with a science PhD
Co-founded SACNAS for diversity in STEM
achievementsCn: 参与首次在细菌中克隆胰岛素基因的团队（1978年）
最早获得科学博士学位的墨西哥裔美国女性之一
联合创立推动STEM多元化的SACNAS

name: Bonnie Bassler
bio: Bonnie Bassler is a molecular biologist who discovered that bacteria communicate through chemical signaling molecules in a process called quorum sensing. Her research revealed that bacteria coordinate group behaviors — including virulence, biofilm formation, and antibiotic resistance — by detecting the density of their population through these chemical signals. This work has opened entirely new approaches to developing antibiotics. She is a MacArthur Fellow, Howard Hughes Medical Institute investigator, and professor at Princeton University.
bioCn: 邦妮·巴斯勒是一位分子生物学家，发现了细菌通过化学信号分子进行交流的过程——群体感应。她的研究揭示了细菌通过检测其种群密度的化学信号来协调群体行为，包括毒力、生物膜形成和抗生素耐药性。这一工作开辟了开发抗生素的全新途径。她是麦克阿瑟奖获得者、霍华德·休斯医学研究所研究员和普林斯顿大学教授。
achievements: Discovered quorum sensing in bacteria
Opened new approaches to antibiotic development
MacArthur Fellow and HHMI investigator
achievementsCn: 发现细菌群体感应
开辟抗生素开发的新途径
麦克阿瑟奖获得者和HHMI研究员
references: Bassler, B. 'How Bacteria Talk to Each Other,' TED Talk, 2009.

name: Martha Chase
tagline: Proved DNA Is the Genetic Material
bio: Martha Chase, together with Alfred Hershey, conducted the famous Hershey-Chase experiment in 1952 that confirmed DNA — not protein — is the genetic material. Using bacteriophages labeled with radioactive isotopes, they demonstrated that only the phage DNA entered bacteria during infection, providing definitive evidence for DNA as the carrier of hereditary information. This was one of the most important experiments in the history of molecular biology.
bioCn: 玛莎·蔡斯与阿尔弗雷德·赫尔希于1952年共同进行了著名的赫尔希-蔡斯实验，证实了DNA——而非蛋白质——才是遗传物质。他们使用放射性同位素标记的噬菌体，证明在感染过程中只有噬菌体DNA进入细菌，为DNA作为遗传信息载体提供了确凿证据。这是分子生物学历史上最重要的实验之一。
achievements: Conducted the Hershey-Chase experiment proving DNA is genetic material (1952)
Used radioactive isotopes to trace DNA and protein in phage infection
Landmark experiment in the history of molecular biology
achievementsCn: 进行赫尔希-蔡斯实验证明DNA是遗传物质（1952年）
使用放射性同位素追踪噬菌体感染中的DNA和蛋白质
分子生物学历史上的里程碑实验
references: Hershey, A.D. & Chase, M. 'Independent Functions of Viral Protein and Nucleic Acid in Growth of Bacteriophage,' Journal of General Physiology, 1952.

name: Mary Lyon
bio: Mary Lyon was a British geneticist who proposed the Lyon hypothesis in 1961 — that one of the two X chromosomes in female mammals is randomly inactivated in each cell during early development. This fundamental concept of X-chromosome inactivation, now known as lyonization, explains many genetic phenomena including why female carriers of X-linked diseases can show variable symptoms. Her discovery has had profound implications for understanding X-linked genetic disorders.
bioCn: 玛丽·莱昂是一位英国遗传学家，1961年提出了莱昂假说——在哺乳动物雌性的每个细胞中，两条X染色体中的一条在早期发育过程中被随机失活。这一X染色体失活的基本概念（现称为莱昂化）解释了许多遗传现象，包括为什么X连锁疾病的女性携带者可能表现出不同程度的症状。她的发现对理解X连锁遗传疾病具有深远意义。
achievements: Proposed the Lyon hypothesis of X-chromosome inactivation (1961)
Concept now known as lyonization in her honor
Transformed understanding of X-linked genetic disorders
achievementsCn: 提出X染色体失活的莱昂假说（1961年）
该概念以她的名字命名为'莱昂化'
改变了对X连锁遗传疾病的理解
references: Lyon, M.F. 'Gene Action in the X-chromosome of the Mouse,' Nature, 1961.

name: Anne McLaren
bio: Anne McLaren was a developmental biologist who pioneered embryo manipulation techniques that laid the groundwork for in vitro fertilization (IVF). In 1958, she and John Biggers successfully grew mouse embryos outside the womb and transferred them back, producing live offspring — a breakthrough crucial to the later development of human IVF. She was a Fellow of the Royal Society and served on the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Authority, helping shape the ethical framework for reproductive technologies.
bioCn: 安妮·麦克拉伦是一位发育生物学家，她开创的胚胎操纵技术为体外受精（IVF）奠定了基础。1958年，她与约翰·比格斯成功在体外培养小鼠胚胎并将其移植回母体，产生了活的后代——这一突破对后来人类IVF的发展至关重要。她是英国皇家学会会员，曾在人类受精和胚胎学管理局任职，帮助建立了生殖技术的伦理框架。
achievements: Pioneered embryo manipulation crucial to IVF development
First to grow mouse embryos outside the womb and produce live offspring (1958)
Fellow of the Royal Society and member of HFEA
achievementsCn: 开创对IVF发展至关重要的胚胎操纵技术
首次在体外培养小鼠胚胎并产生活的后代（1958年）
英国皇家学会会员和HFEA成员

name: Ruth Sager
bio: Ruth Sager was a geneticist who discovered non-chromosomal (cytoplasmic) genes in the alga Chlamydomonas, proving that hereditary material exists outside the cell nucleus in organelles such as chloroplasts. This challenged the prevailing view that all genes reside on chromosomes. Later in her career at Harvard, she pivoted to cancer genetics and made significant contributions to identifying tumor suppressor genes, advancing the understanding of how cells become cancerous.
bioCn: 露丝·萨格尔是一位遗传学家，她在绿藻衣藻中发现了非染色体（细胞质）基因，证明遗传物质存在于细胞核之外的细胞器（如叶绿体）中。这挑战了所有基因都位于染色体上的主流观点。在她后来于哈佛大学的职业生涯中，她转向癌症遗传学研究，在鉴定肿瘤抑制基因方面做出了重要贡献，推进了对细胞癌变机制的理解。
achievements: Discovered non-chromosomal genes in Chlamydomonas
Proved hereditary material exists outside the nucleus
Identified tumor suppressor genes at Harvard
achievementsCn: 在衣藻中发现非染色体基因
证明遗传物质存在于细胞核之外
在哈佛鉴定肿瘤抑制基因

name: Yuan Chang
taglineCn: 发现卡波西肉瘤相关疱疹病毒
bio: Yuan Chang is a virologist at the University of Pittsburgh who, together with Patrick Moore, discovered Kaposi's sarcoma-associated herpesvirus (KSHV/HHV-8) in 1994. This discovery identified the cause of Kaposi's sarcoma, a cancer commonly associated with AIDS. Using representational difference analysis, a novel molecular technique at the time, they isolated viral DNA sequences unique to the tumor. Her work has had a major impact on understanding virus-associated cancers.
bioCn: 张远是匹兹堡大学的病毒学家，1994年她与帕特里克·摩尔共同发现了卡波西肉瘤相关疱疹病毒（KSHV/HHV-8）。这一发现确定了卡波西肉瘤——一种常与艾滋病相关的癌症——的致病原因。她们使用当时新颖的代表性差异分析技术，从肿瘤中分离出独特的病毒DNA序列。她的工作对理解病毒相关癌症产生了重大影响。
achievements: Discovered KSHV/HHV-8, the cause of Kaposi's sarcoma (1994)
Used representational difference analysis to isolate viral DNA
Major contributions to understanding virus-associated cancers
achievementsCn: 发现导致卡波西肉瘤的KSHV/HHV-8（1994年）
使用代表性差异分析分离病毒DNA
对理解病毒相关癌症做出重大贡献
